title: Finding the mean
problem: Find the mean of the data set $5, 3, 8, 4, 10$.

steps.working: There are 5 values in the data set.

steps.instruction: Divide the sum by the number of values.

title: Finding the median and mode
problem: Find the median and mode of the data set $6, 3, 9, 3, 7, 5, 3$.

steps.instruction: Sort the data in ascending order.
Find the median (the middle value). There are 7 values, so the median is the 4th value.
steps.working: The 4th value in the sorted list is $5$. Median $= 5$.

steps.instruction: Find the mode (the value that appears most often).
steps.working: The value $3$ appears 3 times, which is more than any other value. Mode $= 3$.

title: Finding an unknown value given the mean
problem: Five numbers have a mean of $9$. Four of the numbers are $7, 12, 8, 11$. Find the fifth number.

steps.instruction: Use the mean to find the total sum required.
Find the sum of the four known values.
Subtract to find the fifth number.
